title: Anthropic
description: Anthropic Newsroom, Research, and Engineering posts in one feed.

items.title: The Anthropic Economic Index connector
items.content_text: We're launching the Anthropic Economic Index connector for Claude, which lets anyone explore the data directly.

items.title: Supporting ambitious external research through the Anthropic Economic Futures Research Fund
items.content_text: We’re committing $200 million to the Anthropic Economic Futures Research Fund to support ambitious external research.

items.title: Donating another $20 million to Public First Action
items.content_text: Anthropic is contributing an additional $20 million to Public First Action, bringing our total support to $40 million.

items.title: Apply for Anthropic’s AI for Science rare disease research grants
items.content_text: Anthropic is sharing a focused call for AI for Science applications centered specifically on rare genetic diseases. Accepted applicants will receive up to $50,000 in Claude credits over six months, with the goal of building a community of researchers looking into how AI can reshape our understanding of rare disease.

items.title: Introducing Claude for Teachers
items.content_text: Introducing Claude for Teachers

items.title: How Canada uses Claude
items.content_text: How Canada uses Claude

items.title: Anthropic commits $10 million to Canadian AI research
items.content_text: Anthropic is committing $10M to Canadian research institutions to fund the next generation of AI research.

items.title: How Claude's values vary by model and language
items.content_text: We analyzed 300,000 real conversations to measure the values Claude expresses across models and languages, compressed into four interpretable axes.

items.title: How Claude Performs on Robotics Tasks
items.content_text: Do language models’ strengths transfer to robotics? Can a model perceive a scene, understand a particular robot’s state, and issue actions that reliably effect change in the physical world? We ran tests to find out.

items.title: UST is bringing Claude to physical AI
items.content_text: UST is bringing Claude to physical AI

items.title: Ben Bernanke appointed to Anthropic’s Long-Term Benefit Trust
items.content_text: Ben Bernanke appointed to Anthropic’s Long-Term Benefit Trust

items.title: Inviting hard questions
items.content_text: We're asking the public for their hardest questions about AI, and committing to show our work as we address them.

items.title: A new way to reflect on how you use Claude
items.content_text: Introducing a new way to reflect on and refine how you use Claude. It lets you easily track and visualize how you use Claude, and decide whether that time aligns with your goals.

items.title: An off switch for dual use knowledge in AI models
items.content_text: New results on a method of controlling access to potentially dangerous AI capabilities

items.title: A global workspace in language models
items.content_text: Interpretability research on Claude's internal thoughts.

items.title: Government of Alberta uses Claude to find and fix cybersecurity vulnerabilities
items.content_text: The Government of Alberta has been using Claude Code with both Opus and Sonnet models to review its systems, find vulnerabilities, and fix them.

items.title: More details on Fable 5’s cyber safeguards and our jailbreak framework
items.content_text: What is and isn't blocked by our cyber classifiers, and a first draft of our jailbreak severity framework

items.title: Redeploying Claude Fable 5
items.content_text: Anthropic is redeploying Claude Fable 5 starting July 1 following the lifting of export controls, with updated cybersecurity safeguards and a new industry jailbreak framework.

items.title: Claude Science, an AI workbench for scientists
items.content_text: Claude Science is a customizable app that integrates the tools and packages researchers most often use, produces auditable artifacts, and provides flexible access to computing resources.

items.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 5
items.content_text: Our most agentic Sonnet yet, with top-tier intelligence for coding and everyday professional work.

items.title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Cadences
items.content_text: In the latest Anthropic Economic Index report, we look at when people come to Claude, what they produce with it, and how they perceive AI’s impact on their work.

items.title: Introducing Claude Tag
items.content_text: Introducing Claude Tag

items.title: Project Fetch: Phase two
items.content_text: We report results from our latest test of whether Claude can help Anthropic employees perform sophisticated robotics tasks. We found that Claude Opus 4.7, operating without human assistance, was about 20 times faster than the fastest human team at all tasks completed by participants less than a year ago.

items.title: Anthropic opens Seoul office and announces new partnerships across the Korean AI ecosystem
items.content_text: Anthropic opens Seoul and announces new partnerships across the Korean AI ecosystem—with the enterprises, startups, and researchers behind some of the most ambitious deployments of Claude.

items.title: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise
items.content_text: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise

items.title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
items.content_text: The US government has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the United States.

items.title: TCS and Anthropic partner to bring Claude to regulated industries
items.content_text: We’re announcing a partnership with Tata Consultancy Services (TCS). TCS will provide Claude to 50,000 of its own employees across 56 countries; build Claude-powered products for clients in financial services, healthcare, the public sector, and other regulated industries; and join the Claude Partner Network.

items.title: Results from first Anthropic Public Record
items.content_text: Anthropic Public Record is a national survey of attitudes and opinions towards AI.

items.title: DXC will integrate Claude into the systems banks, airlines, and other regulated industries rely on
items.content_text: We’re announcing a multi-year global alliance with DXC Technology, one of the world’s largest IT services companies.

items.title: Introducing Claude Corps
items.content_text: We’re launching Claude Corps, a national fellowship program for people early in their careers who are passionate about extending the benefits of AI to communities across America.

items.title: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
items.content_text: Today we’re launching Claude Fable 5: a Mythos-class model that we’ve made safe for general use.

items.content_text: N days

items.content_text: Paving the way for agents in biology

items.title: Making Claude a chemist
items.content_text: Making Claude a chemist

items.title: Mapping AI-enabled cyber threats
items.content_text: We’ve spent the past year investigating how threat actors are weaponizing AI to conduct cyber operations. Today, we’re sharing a new analysis that maps these real-world attacks onto the MITRE ATT&CK framework, a database of tactics and techniques used by cyberattackers.

items.title: Mapping AI-enabled cyber threats: Insights from the LLM ATT&CK Navigator
items.content_text: Mapping AI-enabled cyber threats: Insights from the LLM ATT&CK Navigator

items.title: Introducing the Services Track and Partner Hub of the Claude Partner Network
items.content_text: In March, we launched the Claude Partner Network, a program for the firms that help enterprises put Claude into production.. Today, we’re announcing two new components that make this ecosystem easier for customers to navigate.

items.title: What we learned mapping a year’s worth of AI-enabled cyber threats
items.content_text: As AI transforms the nature of and methods behind cyberattacks, how well do the techniques and frameworks used by the security community hold up? In a new report, we seek to answer that question.

items.title: Expanding Project Glasswing
items.content_text: We’re extending Project Glasswing to approximately 150 new organizations in more than fifteen countries

items.title: Anthropic confidentially submits draft S-1 to the SEC
items.content_text: Anthropic has confidentially submitted a draft S-1 registration statement to the Securities and Exchange Commission

items.title: Anthropic raises $65B in Series H funding at $965B post-money valuation
items.content_text: Anthropic has raised $65 billion in Series H funding led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital.

items.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
items.content_text: Our latest model, Claude Opus 4.8, is an upgrade to our Opus class of models, with stronger performance across coding, agentic tasks, and professional work, and the consistency to handle long-running work.

items.title: Coding agents in the social sciences
items.content_text: Results from a survey of 1,260 social scientists about AI and coding agent use.

items.title: Anthropic opens Milan office to support Italian enterprise, research, and developers
items.content_text: We're opening a new office in Milan, our sixth in Europe.

items.title: Anthropic appoints KiYoung Choi as Representative Director of Korea
items.content_text: KiYoung Choi is joining Anthropic as Representative Director of Korea, ahead of the opening of our Seoul office.

items.title: Focus areas for The Anthropic Institute
items.content_text: At The Anthropic Institute (TAI), we’ll be using the information we can access from within a frontier lab to investigate AI’s impact on the world, and sharing our learnings with the public. Here, we’re sharing the questions that drive our research agenda.

items.title: Claude is a space to think
items.content_text: We’ve made a choice: Claude will remain ad-free. We explain why advertising incentives are incompatible with a genuinely helpful AI assistant, and how we plan to expand access without compromising user trust.

items.content_text: Learn how code execution with the Model Context Protocol enables agents to handle more tools while using fewer tokens, reducing context overhead by up to 98.7%.

items.content_text: Learn how Claude Code's new sandboxing feature protects developers with filesystem and network isolation, reducing permission prompts and increasing user safety.
